steps.title: Identify angle A
steps.content: The right angle is at B (marked by the square). Angle C = 30° is given. Since angles in a triangle sum to 180°, angle A = 180° − 90° − 30° = 60°. This is a 30-60-90 special right triangle.

steps.title: Apply 30-60-90 side ratios
steps.content: In a 30-60-90 triangle, sides opposite the angles are in ratio 1 : √3 : 2 (opposite 30° : 60° : 90°). Side BC = 36√3 is opposite angle A (the 60° angle), matching the √3 part of the ratio. The scale factor is 36√3 ÷ √3 = 36. Therefore, AB (opposite 30°) = 36 and AC (hypotenuse) = 36 × 2 = 72.

steps.title: Calculate cos A
steps.content:
Using the cosine ratio: $$\cos A = \frac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{AB}{AC} = \frac{36}{72} = \frac{1}{2}$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
